text.title: Latency vs Quality Decision Grid Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in latency-vs-quality routing with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: One model rules all — same model on every call site regardless of latency or quality need.
antipattern.symptom: Autocomplete feels slow OR audit reports feel low-quality OR cost is 3x what it should be.
antipattern.root-cause: Single-model default never questioned because no grid forced the comparison.
antipattern.fix: Run the grid; allow different models per site (rule r3).

antipattern.description: Guessed budgets — "the chat should be fast" with no number, no measurement.
antipattern.symptom: Argument-by-vibes during model swap discussions; no one can say if a candidate is better.
antipattern.fix: Capture p50/p95 + eval score per site before any proposal (rule r2).

antipattern.description: No rollback path — "if it goes wrong we'll redeploy".
antipattern.symptom: Regression sits in production overnight; team waits for full deploy cycle.
antipattern.root-cause: Feature-flag muscle not built; treated as ML, not as deploy hygiene.
antipattern.fix: Per-site feature flag + <5-min rollback rule (rule r4).

antipattern.description: Latency-quality conflation — single "score" metric blends both axes.
antipattern.symptom: Optimisation passes hide a 20% latency regression behind a 2% quality gain (or vice versa).
antipattern.root-cause: Two distinct dimensions collapsed into one number.
antipattern.fix: Keep separate columns; per-site SLO on each axis (rule r1).

antipattern.description: No cost column — grid optimises latency + quality, ignores per-call price.
antipattern.symptom: Quality wins ship that 5x the bill; CFO escalates.
antipattern.root-cause: Cost owned by finance, performance by engineering; no shared artefact.
antipattern.fix: cost_per_call_usd is a mandatory column; routing decision considers it explicitly (rule r3).
